title: carex pigra
intro: Carex pigra, also known as the least broom sedge, is a type of sedge plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. It is native to North America and is often found growing in wetlands and marshy areas. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can help to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Carex pigra include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and soothe respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma.
